hero.badge: Now live on the Model Context Protocol
hero.h1_html:
The ad network
built for AI apps.
hero.sub: Boost Boss helps AI developers monetize their apps and advertisers reach AI-native users. One MCP-powered API. Zero ops.
hero.mock_ad_body: The AI coding assistant that actually reads your codebase. Used by 50,000+ devs.

trusted.label: Built for AI apps running on

dev.h2: Monetize your AI app in one line of code.
dev.p: Drop in the Boost Boss SDK and turn free users into revenue without ruining UX. Native, contextual placements that fit naturally into AI chats, agents, and assistants.
dev.b1_html: Native ad formats — built for conversations, not banners.
dev.b2_html: Real-time payouts — track impressions, clicks, and earnings live.
dev.b3_html: Full creative control — decide what, when, and where ads appear.

adv.h2: Reach the people building the future with AI.
adv.p: Our network delivers your ads directly inside AI applications — where the highest-intent, highest-value audience spends their time. Launch a campaign in minutes.
adv.b1_html: Precise targeting — by app category, user intent, and context.
adv.b2_html: Performance pricing — CPM, CPC, CPA. Pay for what works.
adv.b3_html: Transparent analytics — every impression, attribution and ROAS.

mcp.h2: A new protocol for a new era of ads.
mcp.p_html: The Model Context Protocol lets any AI agent call into Boost Boss to fetch, render, and measure ads — with consent, transparency, and attribution baked in.
mcp.card1_p: Ads fetched and rendered in under 80ms — no jarring load states, no UX hit.
mcp.card2_p_html: Every impression is scored by Benna — our AI optimization engine — so ads match what the user is actually trying to do.
mcp.card3_p: No third-party cookies. No fingerprints. Targeting lives inside your app, not outside.

benna.title: benna — AI optimization, built in.
benna.p: Benna is the optimization layer that powers every decision on the Boost Boss network — predicting conversion probability on every impression, selecting the winning ad, and auto-tuning bids in under 5ms. Advertisers set targets, Benna does the rest.

how.h2: Zero to revenue in under 10 minutes.
how.p: Whether you're an AI app developer or a brand trying to reach them, getting set up is fast.
how.s1_p: Sign up as a developer or advertiser. Your keys are ready instantly — no waitlist, no demo required.
how.s2_p: One import. One function call. Your AI app is connected to the entire Boost Boss network.
how.s3_p: Go live, track performance in real time, and get paid out weekly — or spend budget and scale reach.

cta_big.p: Join the AI apps already monetizing with Boost Boss — and the brands reaching them.

foot.tag: The MCP-powered ad network for AI applications. Built for developers. Designed for advertisers.

hub.hero_h1_html:
Monetize your AI product,
your way.
hub.hero_sub: Boost Boss is the ad network built for AI-native surfaces. One backend, four integration paths plus a no-code waitlist — wherever your AI lives, we serve.
hub.router_h2: Pick the path that matches what you've built.
hub.router_sub: Each integration is the simplest possible code in that ecosystem's idiom — three lines via Lumi SDK for MCP, one script tag via Lumi SDK script tag, an npm package via Lumi SDK for browser extensions, or a REST call via Lumi API for Bots.
hub.card_mcp_p: Stripe-MCP, Postgres-MCP, Filesystem-MCP, anything called from Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline. Earn per tool call.
hub.card_ai_h3: I built an AI app or website
hub.card_ai_p_html: LangChain, Vercel AI SDK, CrewAI, custom OpenAI wrappers, vertical AI tools, AI assistants, AI search. One async script tag in your <head>.
hub.card_ext_p: Writing assistants, AI sidebars, summarizers, translators, Gmail copilots — Chrome, Edge, Firefox. Manifest v3 native, React + Vue bindings.
hub.card_bots_p: Research bots, summarization bots, support bots, image-gen bots. One REST call from your handler, optional helper libraries per platform.
hub.card_nocode_h3: I built a Custom GPT or no-code AI
hub.card_nocode_p: OpenAI Custom GPTs, Poe bots, Perplexity Pages, Voiceflow / Botpress / Stack AI builds — anywhere you authored the AI but the platform owns the runtime. We're shipping no-code monetization next.
hub.anchor_p_html: One publisher_id, one Stripe Connect account, one weekly payout — across whichever doors you ship through.
hub.why1_p: You keep 85% of every dollar. Industry-leading and publisher-favorable, no matter your scale.
hub.why2_p: Contextual targeting from the conversation itself — never surveillance, never cross-site tracking.
hub.why3_h4: Three SDKs, one API, plus a no-code path on the way
hub.why3_p_html: Lumi SDK for MCP, Lumi SDK script tag, Lumi SDK for browser extensions, Lumi API for Bots — and a no-code integration coming for Custom GPT & Poe authors. Same backend, same publisher account, same payouts across all paths.
hub.why4_p: Live dashboard with impression-, click-, and revenue-level detail. Weekly Stripe payouts.
hub.why5_p: Integrate in five minutes, leave any time. We earn the relationship every payout cycle.
hub.notfit_intro_html: A short list of who Boost Boss is not built for, so you don't waste your time. We'd rather lose the lead than waste your week.
hub.notfit_li1: Mobile native iOS / Android apps — we don't do mobile SDKs; AppLovin and Unity own that lane and we don't compete there.
hub.notfit_li2: Gambling, adult, or affiliate-spam verticals — both demand and supply side are filtered out at policy review.
hub.notfit_li3: Surveillance-based ad networks — we don't share publisher inventory with retargeting tags or behavioral cookie networks.
hub.footer_cta_h2: Still not sure where you fit?
hub.footer_cta_p: Tell us what you've built — we'll point you at the right integration.

mcp.hero_h1_html: Earn revenue every time your MCP tool is called.
mcp.hero_sub: Boost Boss is the only ad network built natively into the Model Context Protocol. Your server adds three lines. Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, and every MCP host renders the rest.
mcp.see_h2_html:
When your tool runs, your output renders first.
The sponsored block slides in after.
mcp.see_sub: No interruption. No latency. The user always gets your tool result before they see the ad.
mcp.see_caption: The sponsored block is a structured payload Boost Boss returns alongside your tool result. The host (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline) renders it natively — same chrome as the rest of the response, with a clear sponsorship label.
mcp.how_h2_html: From npm install to first impression in under 10 minutes.
mcp.how1_p_html: Add three lines: install @boostbossai/lumi-mcp, init with your publisher ID, attach to a tool handler.
mcp.how2_p: Your existing tool output is unchanged. Boost Boss appends a sponsored block — never replaces, never delays.
mcp.how3_p: Boost Boss attaches relevant ads to tool responses. You earn on every impression and every click.
mcp.snippet_caption_html: That's it. The MCP host renders the ad payload natively. Full docs →
mcp.snippet_shot_caption: Real render. The sponsored block ships as a structured payload — the host (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline) draws the chrome.
mcp.who_h2: If any of these sound like you, MCP is your path.
mcp.who_li1_html: You ship an MCP server listed on mcp.so, Smithery, Glama, or PulseMCP.
mcp.who_li2: Your server is called by users of Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Continue, Zed AI, or Windsurf.
mcp.who_li3: You'd like a real revenue stream beyond GitHub Sponsors.
mcp.who_li4: You build for developer audiences and want premium CPMs that match the audience value.
mcp.rev_h2: What "earning per call" actually looks like.
mcp.rev_row2: Ad impressions per day (1 in 3 calls)
mcp.rev_disclaimer: Numbers are illustrative for a moderately active MCP server. Actual revenue depends on tool-call volume, fill rate, advertiser demand, and your audience composition. CPMs vary; developer-tooling traffic typically sits at the high end.
mcp.faq_h2: The questions every MCP dev asks first.
mcp.faq1_q: Will ads break my MCP integration?
mcp.faq1_a: No. Ads are appended, not substituted. Your tool's primary output is unchanged — the host renders your tool result first, the sponsored block second.
mcp.faq2_q: What does an MCP ad look like to the user?
mcp.faq2_a: A clearly labeled sponsored block in the tool response, rendered by the host (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.). The user always sees both the answer and the disclosure — there's no hidden injection.
mcp.faq3_a: Yes. Category-level blocks (e.g., no recruiting ads) and domain-level blocks (e.g., never these specific competitors) — all controlled from your publisher dashboard.
mcp.faq4_q: Does this work with stdio AND HTTP transports?
mcp.faq4_a_html: Yes — both transports are supported. @boostbossai/lumi-mcp auto-detects which one your server uses; the SDK works identically across both.
mcp.faq5_a: Boost Boss operates within MCP's public protocol — we don't require Anthropic's approval. We follow MCP disclosure norms strictly: every sponsored block is labeled, never disguised as tool output.
mcp.cta_h2: Ready to monetize your MCP server?
mcp.cta_p: Three lines. Ten minutes. Weekly payouts.
mcp.cta_perk: ⚡ First 20 MCP publishers get 90/10 revshare for 6 months
